title: Volcanic ash and aerosol avoidance for flight routing
summary: Atmospheric forecasts let airlines and ANSPs reroute around volcanic ash and high-aerosol zones safely and efficiently.
description:
Volcanic eruptions and major dust events create dynamic, poorly-bounded hazards across flight levels. Without timely, spatially-resolved ash and aerosol forecasts, airlines and air navigation service providers face the choice between costly blanket groundings and unacceptable safety risk.

CAMS provides global atmospheric composition forecasts — volcanic ash, aerosols and dust — at multiple flight levels, updated several times daily. Integrated into network management and meteorology systems, these forecasts support targeted rerouting and NOTAM issuance, replacing broad airspace closures with precise avoidance envelopes.

site.integration_detail: CAMS volcanic-ash and aerosol forecasts are ingested largely as delivered into aviation meteorology and network-management systems. Dispersion modelling and wind fields refine the picture, but the forecasts are operationally usable as provided.
